question_text: A 500 g ball is dropped from a tall building and eventually reaches terminal velocity. Which statement best explains why the ball stops accelerating?

options.label: A
options.text: Gravity stops acting on the ball once it reaches a certain speed

options.label: B
options.text: The upward air resistance force becomes equal to the downward gravitational force

options.label: C
options.text: The ball's mass decreases as it falls through the air

options.label: D
options.text: The gravitational field strength decreases with distance from Earth

explanation: At terminal velocity, the net force on the ball becomes zero because air resistance (upward) equals weight (downward), so acceleration stops. Gravity continues to act on the ball throughout its fall, but it is balanced by air resistance.
